_metadata.title: Corrective actions and duty of information

published: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 — AI management system (corrective action controls)

obligations.source_quote: Providers of high-risk AI systems which consider or have reason to consider that a high-risk AI system that they have placed on the market or put into service is not in conformity with this Regulation shall immediately take the necessary corrective actions to bring that system into conformity, to withdraw it, to disable it, or to recall it, as appropriate.
obligations.addressee_note: Provider of a high-risk AI system (as defined in Art. 3(3))

decomposed_atoms.description: Provider must immediately take corrective action when non-conformity is identified
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The provider SHALL immediately take the necessary corrective actions to bring the system into conformity, withdraw it, disable it, or recall it

decomposed_atoms.description: Corrective actions include: bring into conformity, withdraw, disable, or recall
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Corrective actions SHALL include bringing the system into conformity, withdrawing it, disabling it, or recalling it, as appropriate

automation_assessment.what_to_scan: Incident response procedures, corrective action plans, withdrawal/recall procedures, disable mechanisms

obligations.source_quote: They shall inform the distributors of the high-risk AI system concerned and, where applicable, the deployers, the authorised representative and importers accordingly.
obligations.addressee_note: Provider of a high-risk AI system

decomposed_atoms.description: Distributors must be informed of non-conformity and corrective actions
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The provider SHALL inform the distributors of the high-risk AI system concerned

decomposed_atoms.description: Deployers, authorised representative, and importers must be informed where applicable
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The provider SHALL inform deployers, authorised representative, and importers accordingly, where applicable

automation_assessment.what_to_scan: Notification procedures, stakeholder communication plans, supply chain notification mechanisms

obligations.source_quote: Where the high-risk AI system presents a risk within the meaning of Article 79(1) and the provider becomes aware of that risk, it shall immediately investigate the causes, in collaboration with the reporting deployer, where applicable, and inform the market surveillance authorities competent for the high-risk AI system concerned and, where applicable, the notified body that issued a certificate for that high-risk AI system in accordance with Article 44, in particular, of the nature of the non-compliance and of any relevant corrective action taken.
obligations.addressee_note: Provider of a high-risk AI system that presents a risk per Art. 79(1)

decomposed_atoms.description: Provider must immediately investigate causes when system presents a risk
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The provider SHALL immediately investigate the causes of the risk, in collaboration with the reporting deployer where applicable

decomposed_atoms.description: Market surveillance authorities must be informed of the risk
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The provider SHALL inform the market surveillance authorities competent for the high-risk AI system of the nature of the non-compliance and any corrective action taken

decomposed_atoms.description: Notified body that issued certificate must be informed where applicable
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The provider SHALL inform the notified body that issued a certificate for that system, where applicable

automation_assessment.what_to_scan: Risk investigation procedures, authority notification protocols, incident reporting workflows
